description: Authoritative DETERMINISTIC cross-implementation reference vector for the ACI E2EE v2 seal/open primitive (secp256k1 ECDH -> HKDF-SHA256 -> AES-256-GCM). Generated and round-trip-verified in Python using the standard algorithm: secp256k1 ECDH shared secret = affine X-coordinate (32 bytes); HKDF-SHA256 with salt=nil, info="aci.e2ee.v2.secp256k1" -> 32-byte key; AES-256-GCM with a 12-byte nonce; Go's GCM appends the 16-byte tag. The ephemeral private key and nonce here are FIXED (not random) purely so the output is byte-reproducible; production seal uses crypto/rand for both. This vector is a deterministic cross-impl arbiter only. The authoritative live-gateway byte match is proven by the live round-trip (Task 6.1), because the real Dstack gateway uses a random ephemeral+nonce per field and ships no static vectors.

source.algorithm: secp256k1 ECDH (affine X-coord) + HKDF-SHA256(salt=nil, info="aci.e2ee.v2.secp256k1") + AES-256-GCM(12-byte nonce, 16-byte tag)
source.reference: Rust src/aci/e2ee.rs::encrypt_for_public_key / decrypt_with_secret_key (k256 raw_secret_bytes == decred secp256k1.GenerateSharedSecret == Python X-coord)
source.generated_by: deterministic Python cross-impl harness (fixed ephemeral + nonce); round-trip-verified

blob_layout: bytes[0:65] = ephemeral pubkey uncompressed (starts 0x04); bytes[65:77] = nonce; bytes[77:] = AES-256-GCM ciphertext+tag
